mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in nonfarm payroll employment reflect underlying economic strength, higher corporate earnings, and improving consumer confidence, which boost S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Spearman rank correlation between monthly PAYEMS change and subsequent 20-day SPX return is less than 0.1 or negative.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate rises above a high threshold, it signals a restrictive monetary policy stance that increases discount rates and suppresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: During episodes when FEDFUNDS > 4.5%, the 60-day forward SPX return is positive more often than 50% over a 5-year walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous strong GDP growth and declining unemployment indicate a robust, non-overheating expansion that supports corporate profits and equity prices. The joint signal of GDP expansion and labor market tightening is more powerful than either alone.
failure_condition: In quarters where GDPC1 rises and UNRATE falls, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of cases over a 10-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: When consumer price inflation is elevated, further increases in CPI force the Fed to tighten monetary policy, compressing equity valuations as real discount rates rise, producing negative forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: When CPIAUCSL is above 200, the subsequent 20-day SPX return is positive in more than 50% of occurrences in a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in housing starts signals robust residential construction activity, a leading indicator of consumer demand and economic growth, which lifts equity prices over the short-term as expectations improve.
failure_condition: On days after HOUST exceeds 2300, the average 5-day SPX return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean (t-test p > 0.05) over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production and retail sales both increase, it confirms synchronized expansion in manufacturing and consumption, a powerful combination that drives corporate earnings and pushes equity prices higher over a quarter.
failure_condition: Months with simultaneous positive growth in INDPRO and RSAFS produce 60-day SPX returns that are lower than the sample median in more than half of occurrences over a 10-year walk-forward.

mechanism_narrative: When industrial production accelerates month-over-month, it signals rising factory output and economic momentum, leading investors to raise growth expectations and push stock prices higher over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: After months when INDPRO monthly growth exceeds 0.5%, the 20-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of cases over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: In a high-unemployment regime, a decline in the unemployment rate represents a meaningful improvement in labor market slack that boosts consumer spending and confidence, translating into strong equity returns over the longer term.
failure_condition: When UNRATE exceeds 5% and subsequently falls, the 60-day SPX return is negative in more than 40% of such episodes over a 10-year backtest.
